steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: "Which choice best describes the overall structure of the text?" means you must match each part of the passage to what each answer says happens, in order. Track three moves: (1) general interest in shipwrecks, (2) a specific study by Rahman et al., (3) a finding about microbial diversity.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It states the number of known shipwrecks, describes the historical significance of one of those shipwrecks, and then comments on the various microbes found at the shipwreck site.: The passage never discusses the historical significance of the Cygne — it is only mentioned by name in passing. The passage also does not comment on "various microbes"; it reports a finding about microbial diversity being tied to pine and oak samples placed about 125 meters from the shipwrecks. ✗
  • Choice B — It introduces a study of microbial communities near shipwrecks that has received significant scholarly attention, summarizes the results of that study, and then describes a research team's reaction to the study.: The passage says nothing about scholarly attention the study has received, and no reaction from the team is described. Both elements are invented. ✗
  • Choice C — It names a famous historical shipwreck, describes the type of wood used to build that ship, and then explains how that wood type influences underwater microbial communities.: The Cygne is not the subject of the study — pine and oak pieces were placed near shipwrecks as an experiment. The passage never describes the Cygne's building materials. ✗
  • Choice D — It notes a general scientific interest in shipwrecks' ecological effects, describes a specific study related to that interest, and then states one of the study's findings.: Matches all three parts: the opening sentence establishes general researcher interest, the middle describes Rahman et al.'s study, and the end gives a finding about sample distance and microbial diversity/richness. ✓
That matches Choice D — It notes a general scientific interest in shipwrecks' ecological effects, describes a specific study related to that interest, and then states one of the study's findings..
